schema_notes: LLM-seeded stub; populated by regen_v3 from primary-source APIs.

person.wealth_source: Ronald Lauder inherited and expanded the Estée Lauder Companies fortune through his family's cosmetics and skincare business, founded by his grandmother Estée Lauder in 1946. His wealth is concentrated in Estée Lauder Companies stock and significant art collection holdings.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes RTB seed; range is ±15% to acknowledge stock-price volatility.
net_worth.liquidity_notes: Wealth primarily held in Estée Lauder Companies public stock (family controls ~50% of voting shares); art collection is illiquid but substantial.

rollup.tier_reasoning: Lauder operates through a traditional foundation but observable giving figures are not yet in the pipeline, making a tier judgment premature. With ~$2 billion in liquid wealth and 30 years as a billionaire, the expectation would be substantial cumulative giving; the absence of public data on foundation payouts or personal contributions prevents any claim about whether he meets, falls short of, or exceeds the 5%-of-liquid norm. A foundation filing review is needed before tier placement.
